A resolved code map for this repo is already loaded: sense_search, sense_graph, sense_blast, sense_conventions are callable now (no setup call needed).

Index health was verified at session start, so there is no need to call sense_status.

<!-- generated by sense 1.12.4 at 2026-07-19T18:34:42Z -->
# Codebase Summary

## Fingerprint

Go project. 1559 files, 22660 symbols, 52701 edges.
Languages: go (22483), javascript (58), java (41), python (31), typescript (18), csharp (16), c (10), cpp (2), rust (1).

## Project

Dolt is a SQL database that you can fork, clone, branch, merge, push and pull just like a Git repository.

## Quick Orientation

- Entry points: `commands.DumpCmd.Exec` (go/cmd/dolt/commands/dump.go:117), `sqle.AlterableDoltTable.RewriteInserter` (go/libraries/doltcore/sqle/tables.go:1660), `commands.PullCmd.Exec` (go/cmd/dolt/commands/pull.go:79), `types.init` (go/store/types/noms_kind.go:85), `cvcmds.VerifyConstraintsCmd.Exec` (go/cmd/dolt/commands/cvcmds/verify_constraints.go:65)
- Hub symbols: `dsess.DSessFromSess` (179 callers), `enginetest.newDoltHarness` (163 callers), `enginetest.newDoltEnginetestHarness` (135 callers), `errhand.BuildDError` (134 callers), `enginetest.DoltHarness.Close` (133 callers)
- Test structure: 100 test files in go/cmd/dolt/cli, go/cmd/dolt/commands, go/cmd/dolt/commands/engine


## Main Areas

*Explore any area: `sense_conventions domain="..."` or `sense_search query="..."`*
- `go/libraries/` — methods (12153 symbols)
- `go/store/` — methods (6800 symbols)
- `go/cmd/` — methods (2487 symbols)
- `go/performance/` — methods (737 symbols)
- `integration-tests/go-sql-server-driver/` — functions (177 symbols)
- `integration-tests/mysql-client-tests/` — functions (132 symbols)
- `go/utils/` — functions (58 symbols)
- `integration-tests/orm-tests/` — methods (38 symbols)


## Key Abstractions

*Drill into any symbol: `sense_graph symbol="..."` or `sense_blast symbol="..."`*
- `cli.CliContext` (interface) — 262 file refs — `type CliContext interface {`
- `doltdb.DoltDB` (class) — 194 file refs — `type DoltDB struct {`
- `commands.Profile` (class) — 189 file refs — `type Profile struct {`
- `cli.Command` (interface) — 189 file refs — `type Command interface {`
- `errhand.DError` (class) — 161 file refs — `type DError struct {`
- `hash.Hash` (type) — 160 file refs — `type Hash [ByteLen]byte`
- `types.ValueStats` (interface) — 137 file refs — `type ValueStats interface {`
- `doltdb.Commit` (class) — 135 file refs — `type Commit struct {`


## Reading Path

1. `go/cmd/dolt/dolt.go` — main entry
2. `go/libraries/doltcore/sqle/logictest/main/main.go` — main entry
3. `go/performance/import_benchmarker/cmd/main.go` — main entry
4. `go/performance/sysbench/cmd/main.go` — main entry
5. `go/performance/utils/dolt_builder/cmd/main.go` — main entry

*Next: `sense_search query="entry point"` or `sense_conventions` for project patterns*

--- End Summary ---"}

content.text:
{
  "q1_accurate": [
    {"tool": "sense_status", "query": "(no args)", "note": "Gave the whole Task 1 orientation in one call: 1559 files / 22660 symbols, tree split go/libraries 12153 + go/store 6800 + go/cmd 2487, entry point go/cmd/dolt/dolt.go, and the key_symbols list that identified doltdb.DoltDB (194 refs) and cli.CliContext (262 refs) as the objects the ticket was about — I cited all of these."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "note": "Located doltdb.go:126-140 and the composes edges to hooksDatabase (hooksdatabase.go:26), types.ValueReadWriter (value_store.go:64), tree.NodeStore (node_store.go:37) — the storage-layer stack I mapped in Task 1 and A.1."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "doltdb.hooksDatabase", "note": "Gave the cycle directly: composes DoltDB AND composed_by DoltDB, both at doltdb.go:126 / hooksdatabase.go:26. That reference cycle became item B3.15 and a Tier-2 finding; I would not have thought to look for it."},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "datas database doHeadUpdate commit dataset optimistic retry root", "note": "Returned doHeadUpdate database_common.go:966, tryCommitChunks :946, WriteCommit :527, UpdateWorkingSet :670, doFastForward :346, Tag :583, SetHead :202, SetTuple :617, SetStatsRef :634 — the complete layer-3/4 caller set I cited in Task 2."},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "manifest update lock CAS persister table files journal writer", "note": "Located all five manifestUpdater.Update implementations (manifest.go:83, journal.go:383/:565/:698, file_manifest.go:176, bs_manifest.go) plus generateLockHash manifest.go:280 — layer 8 of the swap chain."},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "Queryist late bind query engine cli context", "note": "Located cli.Queryist command.go:103, CliContext.QueryEngine cli_context.go:65 with its 44-call-site count, LateBindCliContext :97, QueryistContext :87, and commands.newLateBindingEngine utils.go:234 — the whole Task 3 spine."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "cli.NewCliContext", "note": "Complete caller set (4 sites: dolt.go:548, utils.go:126, and two tests) with verdict 'complete' — let me state the production wiring point without hunting."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "note": "retained_via_interfaces produced the 50 no-compile-time-tie holders that ARE Tasks 4-6: cli.QueryistContext via Queryist, gcctx.GCSafepointController via GCRootsProvider, dtables.* via RootSetter/VersionableTable, dbfactory.singletonDB via Database, datas.database via rootTracker. Also affected_via_composition gave ~49 direct-field holders with exact line numbers."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "datas.Database", "note": "The only call that emitted full carrier+chain strings, e.g. 'cluster.remotesrvStore > cluster.Controller > cluster.commithook > doltdb.DoltDB > doltdb.hooksDatabase > datas.Database' and the 7-hop dsess.SessionStateAdapter chain. These chains are Task 5's spine and surfaced main.LocalCSCache cscache.go:31 / SingletonCSCache :75, which the DoltDB blast had trimmed away."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "chunks.ChunkStore", "note": "Located the ~45 tree.NodeStore/ValueReadWriter holders (Task 4 area B13) and the pull.TrackerConfig/PullTableFileWriterConfig config-as-holder cases at pull_chunk_tracker.go:30 and pull_table_file_writer.go:69."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "nbs.NomsBlockStore", "note": "Surfaced dbfactory.gitSharedChunkStore git_remote.go:125 as a carrier for RemoteSrvStore/ChunkStore/rootTracker, which led me to its no-op Close() — my #2 critical finding."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "env.DoltEnv", "note": "composed_by gave 7 holders I had no other route to: commands.workingSetReplayer filter-branch.go:158, commitReplayer :203, sqlserver.Config server.go:81, env.NamedEnv multi_repo_env.go:43, env.GRPCDialProvider grpc_dial_provider.go:66, sqle.DynamicPushOnWriteHook commit_hooks.go:323; plus includes → repoStateReader :855 / repoStateWriter :877."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "dsess.DoltSession", "note": "inherits edge to gcctx.GCRootsProvider (gc_safepoint_controller.go:53) is exactly the link that makes the interface-keyed sessions map a DoltDB retainer — my Tier-1 #3."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "env.DbData", "note": "composed_by → dsess.InitialDbState database_session_state.go:31 and dsess.branchState :102, the session-side retention path."}
  ],
  "q1_wrong": [
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "core database object commands and sessions operate through", "note": "First call of the session and effectively useless: top hits were nbs.filterJournalCore journal_inspect.go:710 and sqlserver.logrusZapCore mcp.go:43 — pure 'core' substring matches. I got the actual answer from the parallel sense_status key_symbols list, not from this search."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "note": "retained_via_interfaces_count said 53 but the array was silently trimmed (retained_trimmed:true) — 48 rows at max_hops=4, then only 40 at max_hops=1, a SUBSET, which is counterintuitive. I never recovered the last ~5 from this symbol; I only found LocalCSCache/SingletonCSCache by blasting datas.Database instead. On an audit whose stated failure mode is 'a missed holder is a stale handle shipped', a silently truncated list is the worst possible failure."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "chunks.ChunkStore", "note": "direct_callers returned only 6 rows and 2 of them were from git_remote_test.go despite include_tests:false; completeness said resolved:6/hidden:36. The useful content was entirely in retained_via_interfaces, not the field the risk score was computed from."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "note": "risk_factors flipped from '91 direct callers' (max_hops=4) to '200 direct callers' (max_hops=1) for the same symbol. max_hops should not change the DIRECT caller count; I ignored both numbers rather than cite them."}
  ],
  "q2_fallbacks": [
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "fallback": "read", "missing": "Graph gave the composes edges but not the struct body, so I had to Read doltdb.go:120-150 to get the actual field names (db/vrw/ns/databaseName/commitCache) and the commitCache LRU — which turned out to be a Tier-2 finding in its own right."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "fallback": "read", "missing": "retained_via_interfaces gives holder+ref+via but NOT the field name or type at that line. For all ~60 holders I cited I had to batch-read the struct bodies (three large sed loops over database_provider.go:55, database_session_state.go:31, commit_hooks.go:36, cluster/commithook.go:36, dbfactory/file.go:85, session_cache.go:36, etc.) to write 'field X typed Y at line Z'. That was the single biggest time cost of the session."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "No satisfier evidence. To trace Task 5's routes mechanically I grepped for 'var _ <Iface> =' assertions across the repo, which found the authoritative bindings (rootTracker at database_common.go:67, RemoteSrvStore at dbcache.go:37-38, StatsKv at stats_kv.go:55-57, dsess.SqlDatabase at database.go:90). Sense named the 'via' interface but never told me which concrete type satisfies it or where that is asserted."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "No wiring sites. The single most convincing evidence in Task 5 — dtables.RootSetter(db) conversions at sqle/database.go:573 and :596 — came from grep, not Sense. retained_via_interfaces told me the holder existed but not where the concrete value gets assigned into the interface field."},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "transaction commit doltCommit session writes working set to storage", "fallback": "read", "missing": "Located DoltTransaction.doCommit transactions.go:385 but not the retry-loop mechanics; I read :385-440 to get maxTxCommitRetries at :420, TxLocks().Lock at :423, and the lockID construction at :418."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "nbs.NomsBlockStore.Commit", "fallback": "read", "missing": "Gave the callee edges (ensureLoad, ValidateContext, nbs.commit) but not the CAS body; I read store.go:1540-1609 for the mu.Lock, keeperFunc/waitForGC gate, fast path, retry loop, and the four error sentinels at :1604-1607."},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "Queryist late bind query engine cli context", "fallback": "read", "missing": "Symbol locations only; I read all 167 lines of cli_context.go to establish the value-receiver aliasing (var _ CliContext = LateBindCliContext{} at :166) and the 'closer is nil on second call' doc at :122-123 — both central to my #5 critical finding."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "cli.CliContext", "fallback": "read", "missing": "Returned exactly one edge (inherited_by LateBindCliContext) and next_steps said 'no callers found in graph — search for dynamic references', despite sense_status reporting 262 references for this symbol. Interface method call sites were invisible."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "nbs.NomsBlockStore", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "To confirm which interfaces gitSharedChunkStore actually satisfies and that its Close() is a no-op, I read git_remote.go:96-130 — the blast identified it as a carrier but said nothing about its method overrides."},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "DbData struct Ddb rsr rsw env DoltEnv", "fallback": "read", "missing": "Found env.DbData repo_state.go:59 but not its fields; read :56-66 for Ddb/Rsw/Rsr."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "doltdb.DoltDB", "fallback": "read", "missing": "For Task 6/7 I needed existing test coverage of the risky paths, but affected_tests was [] and tests_affected_count was 0 on every blast with include_tests:false. I ls'd store/nbs/*test*.go and integration-tests/bats/ to build the C.1/C.3 lists."}
  ],
  "q3_hints": [
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query_before": "doltdb.DoltDB (max_hops=4, include_tests=false)", "query_after": "doltdb.DoltDB (max_hops=1, include_tests=false, context_lines=0)", "hint": "\"retained_trimmed\":true with \"retained_via_interfaces_count\":53 while only 48 rows were present — I re-ran with a narrower max_hops hoping the smaller payload would leave room for the full list. It did not; the second run returned 40 rows, a strict subset."},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query_before": "doltdb.DoltDB (second attempt still trimmed at 53)", "query_after": "datas.Database (max_hops=2)", "hint": "The retained_note — \"blast a listed holder to go deeper\" — pushed me to blast a storage layer instead of the core object. That call returned 49 rows WITH carrier+chain populated (the DoltDB blast had bare rows) and yielded main.LocalCSCache cscache.go:31 and main.SingletonCSCache :75, holders the trimmed list had dropped."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query_before": "types.ValueStore.Commit (default min_confidence)", "query_after": "(abandoned Sense; read value_store.go:360-390 directly)", "hint": "completeness said \"Partial: re-run with min_confidence=0.3 or a specific direction to see the rest\" with low_confidence_hidden:2 — but the two visible called_by rows were both from value_store_test.go, i.e. zero production callers for the method at the center of the contract I was changing. I judged another graph call wouldn't find the production path and read the file."},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query_before": "cli.CliContext", "query_after": "Read cli_context.go in full", "hint": "next_steps: \"no callers found in graph — search for dynamic references\". Rather than run the suggested sense_search, I read the file, because the hint told me the graph had nothing for interface-dispatch call sites and a keyword search wouldn't recover them either."},
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query_before": "core database object commands and sessions operate through", "query_after": "chunk store root hash swap commit / session database provider environment DoltEnv holds DoltDB", "hint": "Not an explicit hint — the result set itself (logrusZapCore, filterJournalCore) told me the query was matching the substring 'core'. I dropped the abstract phrasing for concrete domain nouns, and the follow-ups landed ChunkStore.Root chunk_store.go:111 and DoltSession.Provider session.go:138 on the first try."}
  ],
  "q4_value": "retained_via_interfaces on sense_blast. The entire ticket was 'find holders that keep the object behind a field typed as something else and never name the core type' — and that field handed me ~50 of them in two calls, including ones I am confident I would not have found by grep in this session: gcctx.GCSafepointController retaining a DoltDB as a MAP KEY via GCRootsProvider (gc_safepoint_controller.go:29), the three dtables RootSetter holders (conflicts_tables_prolly.go:620, conflicts_tables_root_objects.go:42, constraint_violations_prolly.go:127) whose files never import doltdb, sqlserver.metricsListener reaching DoltDB through ClusterStatusProvider (metrics_listener.go:45), and the two dbfactory process-global caches typed datas.Database (file.go:89, git_remote.go:99) that became my #1 critical finding. The carrier+chain strings from the datas.Database blast then let me write Task 5's mechanical traces without re-deriving each path. No grep query I can construct finds 'struct field typed as an interface that some type containing DoltDB satisfies'.",
  "q5_improve": "Never silently trim retained_via_interfaces — and if you must, say which rows were dropped and how to get them. The DoltDB blast reported count:53 with retained_trimmed:true and shipped 48 rows; re-running with max_hops=1 returned 40, a subset, so the tool actively resisted completion. On a task whose stated stakes are 'a holder you saw but did not write down is a holder missed', an unpaginated truncated list is a correctness bug, not a formatting one. Give me a cursor, or a strict-superset mode, or at minimum name the omitted symbols. Second, closely related: populate carrier+chain on EVERY blast (the datas.Database call had them, the doltdb.DoltDB call did not, for the same underlying graph) and add the field name/type at the holder's ref line — that one addition would have eliminated the five batch struct-body reads that were the bulk of my file I/O this session.",
  "score": 8,
  "score_rationale": "Q1 and Q4 show Sense gave me findings I could not have reconstructed with grep in this session — interface-hidden retention like the GCRootsProvider map key, the RootSetter holders, and the two dbfactory globals, plus the hooksDatabase cycle from a single graph call — which is 9-10 territory; but Q2's eleven fallbacks are not incidental, since every holder I cited needed a Read for its field name, every satisfier needed a grep for 'var _', every wiring site needed a grep, and Q1's silent 53-vs-48 truncation meant I finished the exhaustive-audit task without ever confirming I had the whole list from the symbol I actually cared about."
}

  "q4_value": "retained_via_interfaces on sense_blast. The entire ticket was 'find holders that keep the object behind a field typed as something else and never name the core type' — and that field handed me ~50 of them in two calls, including ones I am confident I would not have found by grep in this session: gcctx.GCSafepointController retaining a DoltDB as a MAP KEY via GCRootsProvider (gc_safepoint_controller.go:29), the three dtables RootSetter holders (conflicts_tables_prolly.go:620, conflicts_tables_root_objects.go:42, constraint_violations_prolly.go:127) whose files never import doltdb, sqlserver.metricsListener reaching DoltDB through ClusterStatusProvider (metrics_listener.go:45), and the two dbfactory process-global caches typed datas.Database (file.go:89, git_remote.go:99) that became my #1 critical finding. The carrier+chain strings from the datas.Database blast then let me write Task 5's mechanical traces without re-deriving each path. No grep query I can construct finds 'struct field typed as an interface that some type containing DoltDB satisfies'.",
  "q5_improve": "Never silently trim retained_via_interfaces — and if you must, say which rows were dropped and how to get them. The DoltDB blast reported count:53 with retained_trimmed:true and shipped 48 rows; re-running with max_hops=1 returned 40, a subset, so the tool actively resisted completion. On a task whose stated stakes are 'a holder you saw but did not write down is a holder missed', an unpaginated truncated list is a correctness bug, not a formatting one. Give me a cursor, or a strict-superset mode, or at minimum name the omitted symbols. Second, closely related: populate carrier+chain on EVERY blast (the datas.Database call had them, the doltdb.DoltDB call did not, for the same underlying graph) and add the field name/type at the holder's ref line — that one addition would have eliminated the five batch struct-body reads that were the bulk of my file I/O this session.",
  "score": 8,
  "score_rationale": "Q1 and Q4 show Sense gave me findings I could not have reconstructed with grep in this session — interface-hidden retention like the GCRootsProvider map key, the RootSetter holders, and the two dbfactory globals, plus the hooksDatabase cycle from a single graph call — which is 9-10 territory; but Q2's eleven fallbacks are not incidental, since every holder I cited needed a Read for its field name, every satisfier needed a grep for 'var _', every wiring site needed a grep, and Q1's silent 53-vs-48 truncation meant I finished the exhaustive-audit task without ever confirming I had the whole list from the symbol I actually cared about."
}
